bug_1_dualLogout.title: Dual Logout Handlers & Inconsistent Session Cleanup

rootCause.issue1: Multiple logout entry points without unified cleanup
rootCause.issue2: Frontend logout() calls authApi.logout() but does NOT send refreshToken to backend
rootCause.issue3: AuthContext.logout() catches errors but still clears state - may leave orphaned sessions on failure
rootCause.issue4: Backend logout endpoint doesn't require authentication - allows guest logout
rootCause.issue5: WebSocket and Zustand store cleanup happens after token cleanup - race condition possible

currentFlow.backend: Backend tries to verify refreshToken from req.body but it's missing
currentFlow.cleanup: Frontend clears localStorage, state, store, disconnect websocket

problems: If API call fails, client still clears tokens but server still has them (orphaned session)
refreshToken is never sent to backend for proper revocation
Backend doesn't validate that requester owns the tokens being revoked
No verification that logout actually succeeded before clearing client state

fix.approach: Single authoritative logout flow with proper error handling
steps: Consolidate logout handler to one function in AuthContext
Pass refreshToken to authApi.logout()
Add auth middleware to logout endpoint
Verify token revocation succeeded before clearing client state
Ensure WebSocket cleanup only after token cleanup
Implement proper error recovery (retry with exponential backoff)

bug_2_imageUpload.title: Internal Server Error During Image Upload

rootCause.issue1: No explicit payload size validation in frontend
rootCause.issue2: Base64-encoded images are significantly larger than binary (33% overhead)
rootCause.issue3: Backend routes accept image_base64 but no Content-Length check
rootCause.issue4: Express body parser might have default 100kb limit, base64 images exceed this
rootCause.issue5: Error responses might not be properly serialized when payload parsing fails
rootCause.issue6: No catch block for failed Base64 decoding in face-ai-service

currentFlow.submission: Image sent as image_base64 in JSON body
currentFlow.backend: Express parses JSON → routes access req.body.image_base64
currentFlow.storage: Stored directly in PostgreSQL or sent to face-ai-service

problems: Large image files (> 1MB) converted to base64 (> 1.3MB) + JSON overhead
Express bodyParser.json() default limit is 100kb - request fails at parsing
Error occurs before route handler, so error handler might not catch it properly
Face AI service doesn't validate base64 format before decoding
No progress indication for large uploads

fix.approach: Implement proper payload size handling and validation
steps: Add MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE constant (e.g., 5MB for binary, 7MB for base64)
Frontend validates file size BEFORE conversion
Configure Express bodyParser with explicit limit
Add image compression/resizing before base64 conversion
Validate base64 format before sending to backend
Backend validates base64 format and size before processing
Face AI service validates frames before decoding

bug_3_cameraFrames.title: Camera Fails to Capture Frames Consistently

rootCause.issue1: Closure capture in processFrame might hold stale captureFrameRef.current
rootCause.issue2: captureFrame() in useCamera might fail if canvas.toDataURL() throws error
rootCause.issue3: requestAnimationFrame called regardless of whether capture succeeded
rootCause.issue4: Error in capture loop is swallowed - no indication frames aren't captured
rootCause.issue5: Stream might not be ready when video element tries to draw
rootCause.issue6: Double-mount in StrictMode might start camera twice or cause cleanup races
rootCause.issue7: Video dimensions might be 0 when canvas tries to draw

currentFlow.stream: useCamera creates MediaStream and sets stream state
currentFlow.frame: processFrame calls captureFrameRef.current() every captureInterval ms

problems: captureFrame() called but ref might point to old function (closure issue)
canvas.toDataURL() might fail if video not fully loaded or dimensions are 0
Errors are caught and logged but frame counter never increments
onCaptureRef might be stale if onCapture prop changed
video.videoWidth/videoHeight might return 0 if stream not ready
Multiple instances might fight over camera device (browser locks it)

fix.approach: Fix closure issues and add robust error handling & diagnostics
steps: Ensure captureFrameRef is properly updated on every render
Add video 'loadedmetadata' event listener to verify dimensions
Validate video.videoWidth > 0 before drawing to canvas
Add frame counter increment in onCapture (not in capture function)
Verify stream is ready before attempting capture
Add detailed console logging to track frame capture lifecycle
Implement exponential backoff for capture failures
Handle case where canvas context is null

bug1_logout: Test logout from Navbar - verify all tokens cleared
Test logout from Dashboard - verify all tokens cleared
Test logout with failed API call - verify client state cleanup
Test logout without refreshToken - should still work
Test sequential logouts - should be idempotent
Test logout while websocket connecting - verify no orphaned connections
bug2_imageUpload: Upload corrupted base64 - should fail with clear error
Upload with network timeout - should show error message
bug3_cameraFrames: Camera starts and captures at least 1 frame
Frame counter increments with each capture
Manual capture works independently of auto-capture
Multiple camera devices - can switch between them
Stream is released on unmount (no device lock)
